codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers feature description with two-layer guardrail approach. Primary guardrail in initDiscoveryPrefsModal run button handler blocks at UI level with toast guidance and tab switch. Defense-in-depth guardrail in triggerDiscoveryRun validates payload before webhook emission. Both layers correctly check trimmed values and return early without webhook dispatch.

issues.description: No automated tests added for UI-only guardrail behavior. Worker documented this limitation in handoff: 'No new automated tests added since this is a UI-only guardrail requiring DOM interaction.' Manual browser verification was performed instead. This is acceptable given the UI-only nature but represents test coverage gap.

switchTab('ai') call could silently fail if switchTab function is not defined, but user would still see toast guidance and run would be blocked. Not blocking since the primary guardrail (run blocking + toast) works regardless.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature follows AGENTS.md guidance: 'Run gating must block when both targetRoles and keywordsInclude are empty, and UI must direct users to AI Suggester guidance.' Implementation matches this exactly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: initDiscoveryPrefsModal guardrail checks both fields, shows toast with AI Suggester guidance, switches tab, and blocks run. No deviation from documented guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature also aligns with library/contracts.md: 'Minimum required discovery intent for run dispatch/acceptance: at least one non-empty value in discoveryProfile.targetRoles or discoveryProfile.keywordsInclude.' The defense-in-depth guardrail in triggerDiscoveryRun enforces this at the webhook payload level.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Both guardrails enforce the same blank-check logic, creating consistent behavior between UI and API layers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill (discovery-frontend-worker) procedure step 3 says 'Add or update failing tests first (red) for the changed behavior.' Worker did not add automated tests, justified as UI-only limitation. The skill does acknowledge: 'If no practical automated seam exists for a UI-only change, document that limitation explicitly in handoff.' Worker properly documented this in handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 'No new automated tests added since this is a UI-only guardrail requiring DOM interaction. Manual browser verification confirms...' This follows skill's alternative guidance for UI-only changes.

summary: Feature implementation is correct and complete. Two-layer guardrail (UI-level in initDiscoveryPrefsModal + defense-in-depth in triggerDiscoveryRun) correctly blocks blank-intent runs, surfaces actionable AI Suggester guidance via toast message, and prevents webhook emission. Implementation matches feature description, fulfills VAL-UI-STATUS-008, and follows documented project conventions. No automated tests added for UI-only change, but limitation was documented per skill guidance. Manual browser verification confirms expected behavior.
